_comment: Chronos Earth — curated battles dataset. See public/data/README.md for the schema. 'year' is a signed calendar year (negative = BCE). Casualty figures are rough historical estimates and vary by source. Add a battle by copying an entry. 'commanders' lists each side's leaders: side is 1 or 2 (matching belligerents), wiki is the English Wikipedia article title (used for the portrait + link; run scripts/fetch-portraits.mjs after adding). Set 'hasBattleView': true only if you also add phase data in battle-views.json.

battles.name: Battle of Kadesh
battles.outcome: Tactical draw; led to the earliest known surviving peace treaty.
battles.significance: The largest chariot battle ever fought and the first battle in history recorded in detail. It ended in the world's first known peace treaty.

battles.name: Battle of Marathon
battles.outcome: Persian invasion repelled.
battles.significance: A heavily outnumbered Greek force defeated Persia, preserving Greek independence and inspiring the legend of the marathon run.

battles.name: Battle of Thermopylae
battles.outcome: Greek rearguard annihilated after holding the pass for days.
battles.significance: The famous last stand of 300 Spartans and their allies became the enduring symbol of courage against overwhelming odds.

battles.name: Battle of Salamis
battles.outcome: Persian fleet crushed in the straits.
battles.significance: A decisive naval victory that broke Persia's invasion of Greece and shifted the balance of the Greco-Persian Wars.

battles.name: Battle of Gaugamela
battles.outcome: Persian army shattered; Darius fled.
battles.significance: Alexander's decisive victory ended the Achaemenid Empire and opened the East to Hellenistic civilization.

battles.name: Battle of Cannae
battles.outcome: A Roman army of ~80,000 was encircled and destroyed.
battles.significance: Hannibal's double-envelopment is still taught as the perfect tactical battle, though Rome would ultimately win the war.

battles.name: Battle of Zama
battles.outcome: Hannibal defeated; Second Punic War ended.
battles.significance: Rome's victory ended Carthage as a great power and set Rome on the path to Mediterranean dominance.

battles.name: Battle of Pharsalus
battles.outcome: Pompey routed; he fled to Egypt and was killed.
battles.significance: Caesar's victory in Rome's civil war cleared his path to dictatorship and the end of the Republic.

battles.name: Battle of Actium
battles.outcome: Antony and Cleopatra's fleet defeated.
battles.significance: The naval victory made Octavian sole master of Rome, leading to the founding of the Roman Empire.

battles.name: Battle of the Teutoburg Forest
battles.outcome: Three Roman legions ambushed and destroyed.
battles.significance: The disaster halted Roman expansion east of the Rhine and shaped the boundary between Roman and Germanic Europe.

links.label: Wikipedia: Battle of the Teutoburg Forest

battles.name: Battle of Red Cliffs
battles.outcome: Cao Cao's fleet burned; his southern advance stopped.
battles.significance: A pivotal battle of China's Three Kingdoms period that prevented unification under Cao Cao and is immortalised in legend.

battles.name: Battle of the Yarmouk
battles.outcome: Byzantine army destroyed; Levant lost to the Caliphate.
battles.significance: One of history's most decisive battles, it ended Byzantine rule in Syria and fuelled the early Islamic conquests.

battles.name: Battle of Tours
battles.outcome: Umayyad advance into Western Europe halted.
battles.significance: Often cited as the battle that checked Muslim expansion into Western Europe and secured the rise of the Frankish kingdom.

battles.name: Battle of Hastings
battles.outcome: King Harold killed; England conquered.
battles.significance: The Norman conquest transformed England's language, law and aristocracy — one of the most consequential battles in British history.

battles.name: Battle of Manzikert
battles.outcome: Byzantine emperor captured; Anatolia opened to the Turks.
battles.significance: A catastrophe for Byzantium that began the Turkification of Anatolia and helped spark the Crusades.

battles.name: Battle of Hattin
battles.outcome: Crusader army destroyed; Jerusalem soon fell.
battles.significance: Saladin's victory broke the Crusader states and triggered the Third Crusade.
battles.casualties: Most of the ~20,000 Crusader army killed or captured

battles.name: Battle of Agincourt
battles.outcome: French nobility slaughtered by English longbowmen in the mud.
battles.significance: A stunning underdog victory in the Hundred Years' War that showcased the power of the longbow over armoured knights.

battles.name: Fall of Constantinople
battles.outcome: The city fell; the Byzantine Empire ended.
battles.significance: Gunpowder cannon breached the great walls, ending the Roman/Byzantine Empire after ~1,500 years and marking a turning point into the early modern era.

battles.name: Battle of Bosworth Field
battles.outcome: Richard III killed; Henry VII crowned.
battles.significance: The last decisive battle of the Wars of the Roses, beginning the Tudor dynasty.

battles.name: Battle of Lepanto
battles.outcome: Ottoman galley fleet destroyed.
battles.significance: The last great galley battle halted Ottoman naval dominance in the Mediterranean and boosted European morale.

battles.name: Battle of Vienna

belligerents.side1: Holy Roman Empire & Poland (Jan III Sobieski)

battles.outcome: The Ottoman siege of Vienna was broken by a massive cavalry charge.
battles.significance: Marked the high-water mark of Ottoman expansion in Europe and the start of their long retreat.

battles.name: Battle of Blenheim
battles.outcome: A French army destroyed; Vienna saved.
battles.significance: A landmark victory in the War of the Spanish Succession that ended French dominance of Western Europe.

battles.name: Battle of Poltava
battles.outcome: Swedish army crushed.
battles.significance: Ended Sweden's status as a great power and announced Russia's rise in the Great Northern War.

battles.name: Battle of Plassey
battles.outcome: Bengal fell under Company control.
battles.significance: A decisive (and largely bribery-won) victory that began British political dominance over India.

battles.name: Battles of Saratoga
battles.outcome: A British army surrendered.
battles.significance: The turning point of the American Revolution — the victory brought France into the war on the American side.

battles.name: Siege of Yorktown
battles.outcome: Cornwallis surrendered ~8,000 men.
battles.significance: The decisive siege that effectively ended the American Revolutionary War and secured U.S. independence.

commanders.name: Comte de Rochambeau
commanders.wiki: Jean-Baptiste Donatien de Vimeur, comte de Rochambeau

battles.name: Battle of Valmy
battles.outcome: Prussian advance on Paris halted by an artillery duel.
battles.significance: A morale-defining victory that saved the French Revolution; the Republic was declared the next day.

commanders.name: Duke of Brunswick
commanders.wiki: Charles William Ferdinand, Duke of Brunswick

battles.name: Battle of Trafalgar
battles.outcome: Franco-Spanish fleet destroyed; Nelson killed.
battles.significance: Confirmed British naval supremacy for over a century and ended Napoleon's hopes of invading Britain.

battles.name: Battle of Austerlitz
battles.outcome: The allied army was split and routed.
battles.significance: Napoleon's tactical masterpiece — the 'Battle of the Three Emperors' shattered the Third Coalition.

battles.name: Battle of Borodino
battles.outcome: Russians withdrew; Moscow soon abandoned.
battles.significance: The bloodiest single day of the Napoleonic Wars; a Pyrrhic victory before the catastrophic French retreat from Russia.

battles.name: Battle of Leipzig
battles.outcome: Napoleon decisively defeated and driven out of Germany.
battles.significance: The 'Battle of Nations' was the largest battle in Europe before WWI and broke Napoleon's grip on the continent.

battles.name: Battle of Waterloo
battles.outcome: Napoleon's final defeat; he abdicated days later.
battles.significance: Ended the Napoleonic Wars and a quarter-century of conflict, ushering in a century of relative European peace.

battles.name: Battle of Antietam
battles.outcome: Lee's invasion of the North turned back.
battles.significance: The bloodiest single day in American history; it gave Lincoln the moment to issue the Emancipation Proclamation.

battles.name: Battle of Gettysburg
battles.outcome: Lee's second invasion of the North decisively repelled.
battles.significance: The turning point of the American Civil War and its bloodiest battle, immortalised by Lincoln's Gettysburg Address.

battles.name: Battle of Sedan
battles.outcome: Napoleon III captured with his army.
battles.significance: The decisive battle of the Franco-Prussian War; it toppled the French Empire and led to German unification.

battles.name: Battle of Tsushima
battles.outcome: The Russian Baltic Fleet annihilated.
battles.significance: A crushing naval victory that ended the Russo-Japanese War and announced Japan as a great power.

battles.name: Gallipoli Campaign
battles.outcome: Allied landings failed; eventual withdrawal.
battles.significance: A costly Allied failure that became a defining national moment for Australia, New Zealand and Turkey.

battles.name: Battle of Verdun
battles.outcome: German offensive ground to a halt after months.
battles.significance: The longest battle of WWI and a symbol of the war's industrial-scale attrition ('They shall not pass').

battles.name: Battle of the Somme
battles.outcome: Small territorial gains at staggering cost.
battles.significance: One of the bloodiest battles in history; its first day remains the deadliest in British Army history.

battles.name: Battle of Jutland
battles.outcome: Both fleets withdrew; British blockade held.
battles.significance: The largest naval battle of WWI; though tactically inconclusive, it kept the German fleet bottled up for the rest of the war.

battles.name: Battle of Britain
battles.outcome: The planned German invasion of Britain was called off.
battles.significance: The first major campaign fought entirely in the air; the RAF's defence denied Germany the skies over Britain.

battles.name: Battle of Midway
battles.outcome: Four Japanese aircraft carriers sunk.
battles.significance: The decisive turning point of the Pacific War, ending Japanese naval expansion six months after Pearl Harbor.

battles.name: Second Battle of El Alamein
battles.outcome: Axis forces driven into retreat across North Africa.
battles.significance: A major turning point in the North African campaign — 'the end of the beginning' for the Allies.

links.label: Wikipedia: Second Battle of El Alamein

commanders.name: Bernard Montgomery
commanders.wiki: Bernard Montgomery, 1st Viscount Montgomery of Alamein

battles.name: Battle of Stalingrad
battles.outcome: An entire German army encircled and destroyed.
battles.significance: The bloodiest battle in history and the decisive turning point of WWII's Eastern Front.

battles.name: Battle of Kursk
battles.outcome: The last major German offensive in the East failed.
battles.significance: The largest tank battle in history; after Kursk the strategic initiative on the Eastern Front passed permanently to the USSR.

battles.name: D-Day (Normandy Landings)
battles.outcome: Allied armies established a foothold in occupied France.
battles.significance: The largest seaborne invasion in history opened the Western Front and began the liberation of Western Europe.
battles.casualties: ~10,000 Allied casualties on the first day

commanders.name: Bernard Montgomery
commanders.wiki: Bernard Montgomery, 1st Viscount Montgomery of Alamein

battles.name: Battle of Berlin
battles.outcome: Berlin captured; Hitler died; Germany surrendered.
battles.significance: The final major battle in Europe, ending the Third Reich and the Second World War on the continent.

battles.name: Battle of Megiddo
battles.outcome: Canaanite coalition besieged and defeated at Megiddo.
battles.significance: The earliest battle recorded with reliable detail, and the origin of the name 'Armageddon' (Har Megiddo).

links.label: Wikipedia: Battle of Megiddo (15th c. BC)

battles.name: Battle of Plataea
battles.outcome: The Persian land army in Greece was destroyed.
battles.significance: The decisive land battle that ended the Persian invasion of Greece for good.
battles.casualties: Thousands Greek; tens of thousands Persian

battles.name: Battle of Issus
battles.outcome: Darius fled the field, abandoning his family.
battles.significance: Alexander's first direct victory over Darius, opening the Levant and Egypt to conquest.

battles.name: Battle of the Hydaspes
battles.outcome: Porus defeated despite war elephants; Alexander's eastward limit.
battles.significance: Alexander's last great battle, at the edge of the known world, against war elephants in India.

battles.name: Siege of Alesia
battles.outcome: Vercingetorix surrendered; Gaul was subdued.
battles.significance: Caesar's double-siege masterpiece completed the Roman conquest of Gaul.

battles.name: Battle of Carrhae
battles.outcome: A Roman army destroyed by horse archers; Crassus killed.
battles.significance: One of Rome's worst defeats, exposing the limits of the legion against mobile cavalry.

battles.name: Battle of the Milvian Bridge
battles.outcome: Maxentius drowned in the Tiber; Constantine took Rome.
battles.significance: Led Constantine to embrace Christianity, changing the religious course of the Roman Empire and Europe.

links.label: Wikipedia: Battle of the Milvian Bridge

battles.name: Battle of Adrianople
battles.outcome: Emperor Valens killed and his army destroyed.
battles.significance: A catastrophic Roman defeat often seen as a milestone in the decline of the Western Roman Empire.

battles.name: Battle of the Catalaunian Plains
battles.outcome: Attila's westward advance was checked.
battles.significance: Halted Attila the Hun's invasion of Gaul, one of the last great victories of the Western Roman Empire.

links.label: Wikipedia: Battle of the Catalaunian Plains

battles.name: Battle of Stamford Bridge
battles.outcome: The Norwegian invasion was crushed; Hardrada killed.
battles.significance: Ended the Viking Age in England — days before Harold marched south to meet William at Hastings.

battles.name: Battle of Bannockburn
battles.outcome: A larger English army routed.
battles.significance: Secured Scottish independence for a generation and became a national symbol.

battles.name: Battle of Crécy
battles.outcome: French cavalry shattered by English longbows.
battles.significance: An early, decisive English victory of the Hundred Years' War that announced the dominance of the longbow.

battles.name: Battle of Grunwald (Tannenberg)
battles.outcome: The Teutonic Order's army destroyed.
battles.significance: One of the largest battles of medieval Europe; it broke the power of the Teutonic Knights.

battles.name: Fall of Tenochtitlan
battles.outcome: The Aztec capital fell after a brutal siege.
battles.significance: Ended the Aztec Empire and began Spanish rule in Mexico — a turning point of the Columbian era.
battles.casualties: Tens of thousands (mostly Aztec, incl. disease)

battles.name: Battle of Mohács
battles.outcome: The Hungarian king and army destroyed.
battles.significance: Opened central Europe to Ottoman expansion and ended independent medieval Hungary.

battles.name: First Battle of Panipat
battles.outcome: Lodi killed; Babur took Delhi.
battles.significance: Founded the Mughal Empire in India, partly through early use of gunpowder artillery.

battles.name: Defeat of the Spanish Armada
battles.outcome: The Armada scattered and wrecked on the voyage home.
battles.significance: A pivotal moment that checked Spanish power and bolstered England as a naval nation.

commanders.name: Duke of Medina Sidonia
commanders.wiki: Alonso Pérez de Guzmán, 7th Duke of Medina Sidonia

battles.name: Battle of Naseby
battles.outcome: The main Royalist army destroyed.
battles.significance: The decisive battle of the English Civil War, sealing the defeat of Charles I.

commanders.name: Thomas Fairfax
commanders.wiki: Thomas Fairfax, 3rd Lord Fairfax of Cameron

battles.name: Battle of the Plains of Abraham
battles.outcome: Quebec fell; both commanders died.
battles.significance: Decided the fate of New France and set North America on a British path.

links.label: Wikipedia: Battle of the Plains of Abraham

battles.name: Battle of Culloden
battles.outcome: The Jacobite rising crushed in under an hour.
battles.significance: The last pitched battle on British soil; it ended the Jacobite cause and the clan system.

battles.name: Battle of Jena–Auerstedt
battles.outcome: The Prussian army shattered in a single day.
battles.significance: Demonstrated French military supremacy and brought Prussia to its knees.

commanders.name: Duke of Brunswick
commanders.wiki: Charles William Ferdinand, Duke of Brunswick

battles.name: Battle of Balaclava
battles.outcome: Famous for the doomed Charge of the Light Brigade.
battles.significance: A Crimean War battle remembered for a heroic but disastrous cavalry charge.

battles.name: Battle of Königgrätz
battles.outcome: Austria decisively beaten.
battles.significance: Settled the leadership of Germany in Prussia's favour, a key step toward German unification.

battles.name: Battle of the Little Bighorn
battles.outcome: Custer and his command annihilated.
battles.significance: A famous Native American victory ('Custer's Last Stand') during the Great Sioux War.

links.label: Wikipedia: Battle of the Little Bighorn

battles.name: Battle of Adwa
battles.outcome: The Italian invasion army defeated.
battles.significance: A landmark victory of an African state over a European colonial power, securing Ethiopian independence.

battles.name: First Battle of the Marne
battles.outcome: The German advance on Paris was halted and reversed.
battles.significance: Ended Germany's hopes of a quick victory and began the long stalemate of trench warfare.

links.label: Wikipedia: First Battle of the Marne

battles.name: Attack on Pearl Harbor
battles.outcome: The U.S. Pacific Fleet battleships crippled in a surprise attack.
battles.significance: Brought the United States into World War II.

battles.name: Battle of Leyte Gulf
battles.outcome: The Japanese navy was crippled as a fighting force.
battles.significance: Generally considered the largest naval battle in history; it sealed Japan's naval defeat.

battles.name: Battle of the Bulge
battles.outcome: Germany's last major western offensive failed.
battles.significance: The costliest single battle for U.S. forces in WWII; it exhausted Germany's reserves in the west.

battles.name: Battle of Inchon
battles.outcome: A daring amphibious landing turned the Korean War.
battles.significance: Reversed the course of the Korean War almost overnight by cutting off North Korean forces.

battles.name: Battle of Dien Bien Phu
battles.outcome: The French garrison besieged and forced to surrender.
battles.significance: Ended French colonial rule in Indochina and led to the division of Vietnam.

links.label: Wikipedia: Battle of Dien Bien Phu

battles.name: Annexation of Crimea
battles.outcome: Russian forces seized the peninsula in a near-bloodless operation; the annexation is not internationally recognised.
battles.significance: Opened the Russo-Ukrainian war — the first forcible change of European borders since 1945, prelude to the 2022 full-scale invasion.

battles.name: Battle of Kyiv
battles.outcome: The Russian drive on the capital was fought to a standstill and withdrew after five weeks.
battles.significance: Saved the Ukrainian state in the opening weeks of the full-scale invasion and reshaped the whole war.
battles.casualties: Thousands on both sides; contested figures

battles.name: Siege of Mariupol
battles.outcome: The port city fell after a three-month siege ending at the Azovstal steelworks; the city was largely destroyed.
battles.significance: One of the war's bloodiest sieges — a symbol of resistance and of the war's cost to civilians.

battles.name: Battle of Bakhmut
battles.outcome: After ten months of attritional fighting the ruined city fell; both armies were bled severely.
battles.significance: The longest and bloodiest battle of the war — trench warfare on a scale Europe had not seen since 1945.
battles.casualties: Tens of thousands on both sides

battles.name: Kherson counteroffensive
battles.outcome: Russian forces abandoned the west bank of the Dnipro; Kherson was liberated on 11 November 2022.
battles.significance: The only regional capital taken by Russia in 2022 was recovered — a turning point of the year.
